heading: Disposition of unsold stamps
label: 718b–1 Disposition of unsold stamps
notes: CodificationSection was not enacted as part of act , which comprises this subchapter.Mar. 16, 1934
Prior ProvisionsA prior section 3(b) of , , amended prior to being redesignated 3(c) by .act July 30, 1956, ch. 78270 Stat. 722section 718b of this titlesection 10(i)(1) of Pub. L. 109–266

Amendments2006— inserted section catchline and substituted subsecs. (a) and (b) for former text which read: “On or after , such quantity of migratory-bird hunting stamps, not sold at the end of the fiscal year for which issued, as determined by the Postal Service to be (1) required to supply the market for sale to collectors, and (2) in suitable condition for such sale to collectors, shall be turned over to the Philatelic Agency and therein placed on sale. Any surplus stock of such migratory-bird hunting stamps may be destroyed in such manner as the Postal Service shall direct.”Pub. L. 109–266July 30, 19561971— substituted “Postal Service” for “Postmaster General”.Pub. L. 92–214

children.text: has not been sold by the end of the hunting year (as that term is defined in ) during which the stamp is issued; andsection 718j of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is appropriate to supply a market for sale to collectors; and

children.label: B
children.text: is in suitable condition for sale to a collector.

children.heading: Surplus stock
children.label: b Surplus stock
children.text: The Postal Service or the Secretary of the Interior may destroy any surplus stock of Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamps at such time and in such manner as the Postal Service or the Secretary of the Interior determines to be appropriate.
